tasks.subject: [permission-mode-management] [P1] Expand PermissionModeSchema to 6 values and update runtime capabilities
tasks.description:
Expand the PermissionModeSchema Zod enum from 4 values to 6 by adding 'dontAsk' and 'auto', then update all runtime capabilities to declare all 6 modes.

## Files to modify

### 1. `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts` (line 17-19)

Change the enum from:
```typescript
export const PermissionModeSchema = z
  .enum(['default', 'plan', 'acceptEdits', 'bypassPermissions'])
  .openapi('PermissionMode');
```

To:
```typescript
export const PermissionModeSchema = z
  .enum(['default', 'plan', 'acceptEdits', 'dontAsk', 'bypassPermissions', 'auto'])
  .openapi('PermissionMode');
```

Ordering rationale: ascending autonomy — `default` (most restrictive) to `auto` (most autonomous). `dontAsk` goes after `acceptEdits` because it's a lockdown mode (medium restrictiveness). The `PermissionMode` type automatically expands via `z.infer`.

### 2. `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/runtime-constants.ts`

Change `CLAUDE_CODE_CAPABILITIES.supportedPermissionModes` from:
```typescript
supportedPermissionModes: ['default', 'plan', 'acceptEdits', 'bypassPermissions'],
```

To:
```typescript
supportedPermissionModes: ['default', 'plan', 'acceptEdits', 'dontAsk', 'bypassPermissions', 'auto'],
```

### 3. `apps/server/src/services/runtimes/test-mode/test-mode-runtime.ts`

In `getCapabilities()`, add `supportedPermissionModes` to the returned object:
```typescript
getCapabilities(): RuntimeCapabilities {
  return {
    type: 'test-mode',
    supportsPermissionModes: true,
    supportedPermissionModes: ['default', 'plan', 'acceptEdits', 'dontAsk', 'bypassPermissions', 'auto'],
    supportsToolApproval: false,
    supportsCostTracking: false,
    supportsResume: false,
    supportsMcp: false,
    supportsQuestionPrompt: false,
  };
}
```

### 4. `packages/test-utils/src/fake-agent-runtime.ts`

In the `getCapabilities` mock return value, add `supportedPermissionModes`:
```typescript
getCapabilities = vi.fn<() => RuntimeCapabilities>(() => ({
  type: 'fake' as const,
  supportsPermissionModes: true,
  supportedPermissionModes: ['default', 'plan', 'acceptEdits', 'dontAsk', 'bypassPermissions', 'auto'],
  supportsToolApproval: true,
  supportsCostTracking: false,
  supportsResume: false,
  supportsMcp: false,
  supportsQuestionPrompt: true,
}));
```

### 5. `packages/shared/src/__tests__/relay-binding-schemas.test.ts` (line 65)

The test at line 65 currently expects `auto` to be rejected:
```typescript
expect(() => AdapterBindingSchema.parse({ ...validBinding, permissionMode: 'auto' })).toThrow();
```

Change the test so `auto` is accepted. Update the 'accepts all valid permission modes' test to include all 6 values:
```typescript
it('accepts all valid permission modes', () => {
  for (const mode of ['default', 'plan', 'acceptEdits', 'dontAsk', 'bypassPermissions', 'auto']) {
    const result = AdapterBindingSchema.parse({ ...validBinding, permissionMode: mode });
    expect(result.permissionMode).toBe(mode);
  }
});
```

Update the 'rejects invalid permission mode values' test to remove `auto` from the rejected list:
```typescript
it('rejects invalid permission mode values', () => {
  expect(() => AdapterBindingSchema.parse({ ...validBinding, permissionMode: 'yolo' })).toThrow();
  expect(() => AdapterBindingSchema.parse({ ...validBinding, permissionMode: '' })).toThrow();
});
```

## Acceptance criteria

- `PermissionModeSchema.parse('dontAsk')` returns `'dontAsk'`
- `PermissionModeSchema.parse('auto')` returns `'auto'`
- `PermissionModeSchema.parse('yolo')` throws
- All 4 runtime capability sources include all 6 modes
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- `pnpm test -- --run` passes (after relay-binding-schemas test update)
tasks.activeForm: Expanding PermissionModeSchema to 6 values and updating runtime capabilities

tasks.subject: [permission-mode-management] [P3] Wire supportedModes from useDefaultCapabilities into ChatStatusSection
tasks.description:
Pass `supportedPermissionModes` from the runtime capabilities to the `PermissionModeItem` component via the `ChatStatusSection`.

## File to modify

### `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/status/ChatStatusSection.tsx`

#### 1. Add the capabilities import:
```typescript
import { useDefaultCapabilities } from '@/layers/entities/runtime';
```

This import is at the entities layer, which is allowed from a features-layer component per FSD rules.

#### 2. Inside the `ChatStatusSection` component, call the hook:
```typescript
const capabilities = useDefaultCapabilities();
```

Place this near the other data hooks at the top of the component (around line 127, near `const status = useSessionStatus(...)`).

#### 3. Pass `supportedModes` to `PermissionModeItem`:

Find the existing `PermissionModeItem` usage (around line 266-269):
```typescript
<PermissionModeItem
  mode={status.permissionMode}
  onChangeMode={(mode) => status.updateSession({ permissionMode: mode })}
  disabled={!sessionId}
/>
```

Add the `supportedModes` prop:
```typescript
<PermissionModeItem
  mode={status.permissionMode}
  onChangeMode={(mode) => status.updateSession({ permissionMode: mode })}
  disabled={!sessionId}
  supportedModes={capabilities?.supportedPermissionModes}
/>
```

The `useDefaultCapabilities()` hook already exists in `apps/client/src/layers/entities/runtime/model/use-runtime-capabilities.ts` with `staleTime: Infinity` — it caches indefinitely since capabilities don't change during a server process lifetime. When capabilities are still loading (`undefined`), the `PermissionModeItem` will show all modes (its default behavior when `supportedModes` is undefined).

## Acceptance criteria

- `PermissionModeItem` receives `supportedModes` from runtime capabilities
- While capabilities are loading, all modes are shown (graceful degradation)
- Once capabilities load, only modes the runtime supports are shown
- No new API calls are made (capabilities are already cached)
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
tasks.activeForm: Wiring supportedModes from useDefaultCapabilities into ChatStatusSection
